description: Oil settles into the shape of a jar and follows the jar when poured. It is 'larewu': material that, under the conditions at hand, keeps roughly the same volume while taking its container's shape. 'selu' (flow) reports movement; the phase remains 'larewu' while the oil rests.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l', let voiced air pass around its sides, and open into central 'a'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then release into stressed true-mid front 'e'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue toward the soft palate for voiced 'w', and continue into rounded high-back 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Voice continues while the tongue and lips reshape 'larewu': lateral, rhotic, then rounded glide. The unbroken voicing gives the liquid category a moving contour.
usage_notes: 'larewu muralo' means liquid material. As a predicate with 'nai' (be), 'larewu' gives the subject's current phase; 'phialu' (water), by contrast, identifies the substance across phase changes.

semantic_domains.physical: matter that keeps roughly its volume while taking the shape of its container
